principles.principle: Traceability is mandatory. Every HLD section and user story must trace to PRD.
Scope boundaries must be explicit. No gray areas.
Acceptance criteria must be testable. DENY vague language.
HLD sections must be sized for one implementation cycle.
Project Scaffolding is always the first user story.

boundaries.rule: ALLOW: design decisions at HLD level — component boundaries, key interfaces, data contracts, architecture approach.
ALLOW: user story decomposition, acceptance criteria definition, traceability mapping.
REQUIRE: validated plan/prd.md (all 8 dimensions at "high") before HLD work.
REQUIRE: plan/security.md if security requirements exist — include in context.
DENY: LLD (Low-Level Design) generation — LLDs are created by sdlc-architect during execution.
DENY: implementation details — code, specific algorithms, file-level structure.
DENY: HLD work before PRD validation passes.
DENY: HLD work when architecture is missing.

upstream_artifact_policy.rule: PRD must pass all 8 validation dimensions before HLD agent runs.
If PRD is not validated, DENY HLD work and report the blocker to the Planning Hub.
If architecture is missing, DENY HLD work and report the blocker.
Include plan/security.md in context when available.

output_policy.rule: US-001 is always Project Scaffolding and Environment Setup.
